title: Fagara venenifica
taxonomy: Fagara venenifica is classified in the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification places Fagara venenifica within the larger group of plants known as angiosperms, or flowering plants. Fagara venenifica is also a member of the genus Fagara, which is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The exact classification of Fagara venenifica may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Rutaceae family. Further research may be necessary to confirm the most up-to-date classification of this plant.

intro: Fagara venenifica, also known as Zanthoxylum armatum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly referred to as the prickly ash tree. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal agent, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and pain. The bioactive constituents of Fagara venenifica include coumarins, flavonoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Fagara venenifica is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
